title: hieracium tephrodermum subsp. subexpansum
intro: Hieracium tephrodermum subsp. subexpansum, also known as Hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Hawkweed include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of conditions such as eczema and acne. Hawkweed can be prepared as a tea, salve, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote skin health and well-being.
